sources.notes: Statement and constraints checked: t <= 1500, n <= 3000, sum n^2 <= 9e6, and 0 <= a_i,b_i <= 1e9; one minimum simple-path cost is required for every cardinality 2..n.

Official editorial page checked. Its prose remained lazy-loaded, but both reference implementations were readable and confirm sorting by a_i-b_i (or the reverse orientation) followed by a constant-state exact-cardinality DP.

Cross-checked the max-to-absolute-value transform, the fixed-endpoint line-walk formula, the four ordered critical roles, and an O(n^2) DP. The displayed B_i definition has an apparent subscript typo; its formula and code use a_i-b_i.
Cross-checked the equivalent b_i+b_j+max(c_i,c_j) rewrite and the three-phase DP transitions after sorting c_i=a_i-b_i.
Cross-checked the endpoint/extrema derivation: fixed selected set and endpoints reduce the absolute-difference contribution to 2(max q-min q)-abs(q_s-q_t).

Independently derived the line-metric formula and role contributions, then matched a rolling role-DP implementation against brute-force enumeration on 515 random cases with n=2..7, including tied q_i values.

annotation.summary: For each k from 2 to n, choose k cities and order them as a simple path minimizing the sum of max(a_i+b_j,b_i+a_j) over adjacent pairs.
annotation.constraints: t <= 1500; 2 <= n <= 3000; sum n^2 <= 9e6; 0 <= a_i,b_i <= 1e9. Quadratic time per test is intended, linear rolling memory is possible, and all costs require 64-bit integers.
annotation.core_idea: Set p_i=a_i+b_i and q_i=a_i-b_i, so twice an edge cost is p_i+p_j+abs(q_i-q_j). For a selected set S with endpoints s,t, reverse the path if needed so q_s<=q_t. If m is the minimum selected q and M is the maximum selected q, the minimum total absolute-difference part is 2(M-m)-(q_t-q_s): go from s down to m, visit all remaining cities in nondecreasing q order up to M, then go down to t. Hence 2C=2*sum_{v in S}p_v-p_s-p_t+2(M-m)-(q_t-q_s). The only nonuniform roles are therefore ordered as m <= s <= t <= M. Sort by q and run an exact-cardinality selection DP: every selected city has base contribution 2p, while assigning the min, start, end, and max roles adds -2q, -p+q, -p-q, and +2q respectively. A constant phase records how many of min/start/end have been assigned; the max role is applied when an answer is finalized. The min may equal the start and the end may equal the max, which handles boundary cases such as k=2.
annotation.complexity: O(n^2) time and O(n) extra memory per test after O(n log n) sorting; O(sum n^2) total time under the input bound.
tricks: rewrite max of two cross-sums as a linear term plus one absolute difference
reverse the undirected path so the start coordinate is at most the end coordinate
replace arbitrary path permutations on a line by a span-and-endpoints formula
encode min, start, end, and max as four ordered roles that may coalesce only at min=start and end=max
keep doubled costs throughout the DP to avoid fractions
review_risks: The official editorial prose was not exposed by the page renderer; only its reference code was readable, so the proof was reconstructed from independent sources and self-derivation.

The implementation is sensitive to sorting by q_i=a_i-b_i rather than p_i, and to allowing min=start and end=max while still selecting distinct start/end cities; equal-q ties require careful but otherwise arbitrary ordering.
The role-transition signs were brute-force checked on small random instances, but the main agent should still re-check those signs and 64-bit bounds before applying the draft.

solution_variants.name: sorted-four-role-cardinality-dp
solution_variants.summary: Sort cities by q_i. In doubled costs, treat 2p_i as the ordinary contribution of a selected city and assign the ordered min/start/end/max adjustments with a three-phase rolling DP over the selected count. Initialize both min-only and min=start states; selecting later cities either leaves the phase unchanged, assigns start, assigns end, or finalizes max, so all answers for k=2..n are obtained in one quadratic scan. The common three-state b_i+max(c_i,c_j) formulation is algebraically the same algorithm, not a materially distinct variant.
solution_variants.complexity: O(n^2) time and O(n) extra memory after sorting.

tags.description: Optimize walks over scalar coordinates using total variation and span identities, especially endpoint-to-endpoint paths that must visit every selected point on a line.
tags.created_reason: Existing geometry and Manhattan-transform tags capture the coordinate rewrite but not the reusable identity that a fixed-endpoint walk visiting scalar points costs 2(max-min)-endpoint_distance. This distinction improves retrieval for one-dimensional route-optimization problems. Candidate for main-agent taxonomy review.
tags.evidence: After the coordinate transform, the decisive optimization is the line-metric identity 2(M-m)-abs(q_s-q_t), which removes the path permutation and leaves only extrema and endpoint roles.
The O(n^2) algorithm selects each sorted city or skips it while a constant phase records whether min, start, and end roles have been assigned; selected count supplies every requested k.
Sorting by q_i=a_i-b_i turns the required order min <= start <= end <= max into a forward scan, making the finite-state cardinality DP possible.
The diagonal coordinates p_i=a_i+b_i and q_i=a_i-b_i convert max(a_i+b_j,b_i+a_j) into (p_i+p_j+abs(q_i-q_j))/2.
